short: Training a small student model to copy the outputs of a larger teacher model.
definition: Knowledge distillation transfers capabilities from a large teacher model to a smaller student model by training the student to match the teacher's outputs, its internal working values, or both. The teacher does not just give one right answer; it reports how likely each option is, and those graded probabilities carry richer information than a single correct label because they reveal how confident it is. It is widely used to shrink large models for cheaper or on-device use while keeping much of the original performance.
